codeReview.summary: The feature adds rollback/tag command wiring, output formatting, and focused tests for the main success and failure paths. The core requested behaviors are present, but I found two edge-case correctness gaps around argument parsing and empty host-filter handling.

issues.description: `parseArgs()` only records positional arguments when the token does not start with `-` (`apps/cli/src/cli.ts:93-109`), so rollback/tag inputs such as `--bogus` or `-bad` are swallowed as flags before they can reach `GitOps.checkoutRef()` / `createTag()`. Reproduced with `node /home/shuv/repos/codex-fleet/apps/cli/bin/fleet.js rollback --bogus` and `node /home/shuv/repos/codex-fleet/apps/cli/bin/fleet.js tag -bad`, both of which return `missing required argument` instead of surfacing a clear invalid-ref/tag error. That leaves the feature's invalid-input handling incomplete for leading-dash names and bypasses the git-ops layer's explicit validation path.

When `filterHosts` is provided but none of the requested names exist, `targetHosts` becomes empty, `Effect.all([])` yields an empty array, and `results.every(...)` marks the operation as `allSucceeded: true` (`apps/cli/src/commands/rollback.ts:101-115`; same logic in `apps/cli/src/commands/tag.ts:101-115`). I reproduced this with `Effect.runPromise(runRollback(..., ["missing-host"]))` and `runTag(...)`, both returning `{ hosts: [], allSucceeded: true }`. Because `runRollbackCommand()` / `runTagCommand()` return exit code 0 whenever `allSucceeded` is true, a typoed host filter silently reports success even though no host was touched.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `cli-worker` skill's `followedProcedure` signal is inaccurate for this run. The skill requires writing tests before implementation and doing manual CLI verification, but this worker implemented the new command files first and the handoff only records automated validators/build steps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/cli-worker/SKILL.md:28-30,81-90` says to create tests BEFORE implementation and then manually verify the real CLI. The transcript skeleton for session `c33ff47c-bea3-4524-a1ce-953e5bd7eccb` shows `apps/cli/src/commands/rollback.ts` and `apps/cli/src/commands/tag.ts` being created before `apps/cli/test/rollback.test.ts` and `apps/cli/test/tag.test.ts`. The handoff's `verification.commandsRun` only lists `npm test -- --filter @codex-fleet/cli`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` (`.../handoffs/2026-03-15T22-23-29-796Z__cli-rollback-tag__c33ff47c-bea3-4524-a1ce-953e5bd7eccb.json:16-37`), yet the same handoff records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` (`.../handoffs/2026-03-15T22-23-29-796Z__cli-rollback-tag__c33ff47c-bea3-4524-a1ce-953e5bd7eccb.json:169-170`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library does not capture an important testing nuance for git/SSH command failures: `SshExecutorTest` does not convert a `_tag: "result"` with a non-zero `exitCode` into `CommandFailed`, so tests must model those cases with `_tag: "error"` and an explicit `CommandFailed` value.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The reviewed transcript skeleton for session `c33ff47c-bea3-4524-a1ce-953e5bd7eccb` includes the worker's debugging note: 'The mock SSH executor returns the `CommandResult` directly when `_tag: "result"`, regardless of the exit code,' after an initial rollback/tag test failure. `.factory/library/architecture.md` mentions the `CommandFailed` type, but none of `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md`, or `.factory/library/user-testing.md` explain this mock-behavior difference, so workers currently have to rediscover it while writing tests.

summary: Pass with two non-blocking issues. The rollback/tag feature covers the main required behaviors and has solid focused tests, but leading-dash arguments are misparsed before validation and typoed host filters can currently report a false success with zero hosts processed.
